content.text: Authenticity & Trust: FinTag helps businesses and financial institutions provide verified payment details directly in their website’s metadata. This can prevent fraud and scams by ensuring that visitors and automated services retrieve official payment details instead of falling prey to phishing attacks.

Automation & Integration: Browser extensions, payment apps, and digital services can auto-detect FinTags on a webpage, enabling seamless transactions without manual input errors. For instance, a banking extension could automatically recognize and pre-fill a payment form using the information in FinTags.

Decentralized & Open-Source: The FinTag standard is flexible and open-source, allowing businesses, nonprofits, and individuals to integrate it into any website. Each subpage can include different payment details, making it a robust solution for large organizations with multiple departments or divisions.
Improved Payment Experience: Clients and customers can instantly verify payment details before initiating a transfer, reducing the chances of incorrect payments or fraudulent transactions.
Universal Standardization: FinTag can serve as a universal financial metadata standard, making online transactions more transparent, efficient, and secure.

Enhanced Security & Privacy
  • Instead of exposing sensitive payment details like IBAN, UPI, or BIC, the PKPASS file encapsulates payment instructions securely.
  • Reduces the risk of fraud, phishing, and unauthorized access.

Seamless Wallet Integration
  • PKPASS files are natively supported by Apple Wallet and Google Wallet, making it easy to import and initiate payments directly from a mobile device.
  • Transactions become contactless and digital, eliminating the need for paper invoices or manual bank transfers.

Flexibility and Interoperability
  • PKPASS files support multiple payment methods such as ICAN, IBAN, ACH, PIX, UPI, BIC, and more.
  • They can be customized with QR codes, barcodes, or NFC triggers for easier processing.

Effortless Payment Requests
  • Businesses can generate and send a PKPASS payment request to customers who simply import it into their wallets and approve the payment.
  • Ideal for invoices, online orders, or peer-to-peer transfers.

Organization Code (A-Z) – 4 Letters
  • Identifies the VASP provider and is usually a shortened version of its name.
  • Example: Ping for Ping Exchange.

Country Code (A-Z) – 2 Letters
  • Represents the country where the VASP is registered.
  • Follows ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes.
  • Example: US for the United States,CH for Switzerland.

Digital Address Identifier (0-9, A-Z) – 2 Characters
  • Specifies the type of digital asset address and network participation.
  • Format: XY where:
    • X (Letter or Number) – General location code.
    • Y (Number) – Specifies the type of usage:
      • 0 = Test environment address.
      • 1 = Passive network participant. (Not in custody of wallet)
      • 2 = Reverse billing (recipient pays for the transaction - deducted transaction fees).
    • Example: A1 (Live address, passive participant), B0 (Test network address).

Asset Code (0-9, A-Z) – 3 Characters
  • Represents the supported digital asset or currency.
  • Can be:
    • Specific crypto assets (BTC, ETH, XCB).
    • Fiat currencies (CBDC, Stable, MiCA) (EUR, CHF).
    • Multi-currency support (XXX).
  • Example: BTC (Bitcoin asset), XXX (multi-currency VASP).
Standardized Crypto Transactions. ICAN simplifies cryptocurrency payments by using a uniform format, making transactions across different platforms and services seamless.
Error Prevention & Security. The built-in checksum automatically detects errors in address input, preventing transaction failures and loss of funds.
Universal Compatibility. Designed for wallets, exchanges, and financial institutions, ICAN enhances interoperability, ensuring your crypto transactions are recognized and processed everywhere.

Reliable Transaction Messaging. TxMS ensures that important transaction details are correctly transmitted, preventing errors and miscommunication. This is especially useful for banks, digital wallets, and blockchain applications.
Secure and Standardized Format. TxMS follows a universal standard for financial messaging, ensuring that messages are always readable and compatible across different platforms.

Perfect for Digital Payments. With TxMS, payment instructions, confirmations, and invoices can be sent in a way that’s easy to process and verify — even over traditional communication channels like SMS or MMS.
Reduces Errors & Fraud. By using TxMS, businesses and users can be confident that the payment details they receive are accurate, reducing the risk of fraud or sending money to the wrong account.
